meta.name: Beef Shawarma
meta.subtitle: Thin-sliced, warmly spiced beef with shawarma char—oven, skillet, or grill options

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalogs
notes: Designed for home kitchens without a vertical spit/rotisserie.
Includes fast weeknight path (skillet) and big-batch sheet-pan path.

summary: Classic beef shawarma flavors—garlic, lemon, and warm spices—marinated and cooked hot for browned edges, then sliced for wraps, bowls, and plates.

yield.notes: 3 lb beef makes ~6 hearty wraps/bowls (or 8 smaller servings).

heat.note: Add cayenne or chili flakes if you want more heat.

optional.label: Grill
optional.notes: Adds smoke and fast browning; best for larger strips.

optional.label: Food processor
optional.notes: Optional for quick onion-garlic paste style marinade.

notes: No dairy required in the base recipe.
If serving with tahini sauce, check sesame allergen considerations.

ingredients.label: Beef (sirloin, flank, or top round), thinly sliced
notes: Best choices: sirloin (tender), flank/skirt (strong beef flavor), top round (lean—don’t overcook).
For easiest slicing: freeze 20–30 minutes, then slice thinly across the grain.

ingredients.label: Yellow onion, grated or very finely minced
ingredients.notes: Grating gives a shawarma-shop style onion-juice marinade.

ingredients.label: Ground cloves
ingredients.notes: A tiny pinch goes a long way—optional but classic in some shawarma blends.

ingredients.label: White vinegar or apple cider vinegar
ingredients.notes: Optional brightness; common in some regional shawarma marinades.

steps.title: Slice the beef thinly
instructions: If needed, chill beef in the freezer for 20–30 minutes to firm it up.
Slice very thinly across the grain (aim for 1/8-inch).
If slices are long, cut into bite-size strips for easier cooking and serving.
checks: Slices are thin and mostly uniform for even cooking.
Sliced across the grain for tenderness.

steps.title: Mix marinade
instructions: In a large bowl, combine olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, and grated onion.
Add salt, pepper, cumin, coriander, paprika, cinnamon, and allspice.
Add optional clove, cardamom, cayenne, and vinegar if using. Mix well.
checks: Spices are fully blended; no dry clumps.

steps.title: Marinate the beef
instructions: Add sliced beef to the bowl and toss until evenly coated.
Cover and refrigerate at least 30 minutes (ideal: 2–8 hours).
If marinating overnight, keep salt as written (it’s fine), but don’t exceed 18 hours for best texture.
checks: Beef is evenly coated; marinade is clinging to slices.

steps.title: Cook (sheet-pan, oven method)
instructions: Heat oven to 450°F. Place a rack in the upper third of the oven.
Line a sheet pan. Spread beef slices in a single layer (use 2 pans if needed).
Roast 8–12 minutes until browned at edges. For more char, broil 1–2 minutes at the end (watch closely).
Don’t overcook—thin beef should stay tender.

temperature.notes: High heat mimics shawarma spit browning; avoid steaming by not overcrowding.

checks: Edges are browned; beef is still tender.
If juices pool heavily, pan was overcrowded—use two pans next time.

steps.title: Cook (fast skillet method)
instructions: Heat a cast-iron or heavy skillet over high heat until very hot.
Add a small amount of oil if needed (often the marinade has enough).
Cook beef in batches in a single layer, 60–90 seconds per side, until browned.
Transfer cooked batches to a plate; repeat. Combine at the end.
checks: Beef is browned quickly (seared), not simmered.

steps.title: Serve
instructions: Wrap: Spread tahini sauce on warm pita/flatbread, add beef, pickled turnips/pickles, tomatoes, cucumbers, and parsley.
Bowl: Serve over rice, fries, or chopped salad with sauce.
Plate: Serve with roasted vegetables and a bright lemony salad.
checks: Taste for brightness—add lemon or pickles if needed.

refrigerate.notes: Reheat in a hot skillet to re-crisp edges. Microwave reheating can soften texture.

freeze.notes: Freeze cooked beef in flat bags. Reheat thawed in a skillet for best results.

weeknightTips: Slice and marinate in the morning; sheet-pan roast at dinner time.
Use the skillet method when you only need 2–3 servings.
scalingNotes: For double batches, use two sheet pans or cook in skillet batches to prevent steaming.

notes: Ingredient IDs are SSA-style and can be mapped via ingredient alias catalogs.
If your ingredient catalog uses different IDs (e.g., beef.sirloin), map accordingly.
Thin slicing is the biggest success factor. If slices are too thick, the beef can turn chewy.
If using very lean beef (top round), do not broil long—cook just until browned.
For a more 'shawarma shop' aroma, add 1 tsp ground sumac (optional) to the marinade.
If using strips, thread onto skewers for easier grilling.
Grill over high heat just to brown, then slice and serve.
Avoid overcooking—beef should stay tender.
Air fry at 400°F for 6–10 minutes, shaking halfway, until edges are browned.
Cook in a single layer; do multiple batches if needed.
Increase cayenne to 1 tsp and add 1 tsp crushed red pepper.
Serve with a spicy garlic sauce or hot sauce.
Skip grated onion and use 1 tsp onion powder instead.
Add 1 extra tbsp lemon juice for brightness.
Marinate beef in the refrigerator.
If cooking on a sheet pan, avoid overcrowding to prevent steaming and uneven cooking.
Discard marinade that contacted raw beef unless boiled.
